notes: MB search match (sim 1.0); title/artist exact. All 6 keyTracks present in MB tracklist ('Susie (2nd take)' matches 'Susie (Take A/B)', take suffix ignored). MB release is a 1974 Milestone compilation of 1920s recordings; ours year 1926 and label 'Unknown' are not contradicted by evidence. 'contemporary jazz' is an odd editorial label, not a factual claim.

Title 'Djangology'. MB search match (sim 1.0): Django Reinhardt, Jazz Vogue VJD502, dated 1974 (ours year 1949, label 'Vogue' ~ 'Jazz Vogue'). Of the 4 keyTracks, Djangology/Nuages/Swing 39 are in the MB tracklist but 'Minor Swing' is NOT — the DNA also names 'Minor Swing' as included. However the MB match is a later 1974 reissue (different year than ours 1949), so its 24-track listing may not reflect the original release the entry describes; 'Minor Swing' is Django's most famous tune and appears on many Djangology compilations. Per absence-is-not-contradiction and MB-search-reissue not being authoritative on the original's contents, not treated as a confident contradiction. Flagged as keyTracks mismatch for a reconciler to verify against the specific edition.

MB embedded_mbid: Lester Young, Norgran MGN 1005, 1954 — matches DNA. All 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. Raised in notes per absence-is-not-contradiction; a reconciler may want to soften the 'bebop' era framing. ours.wikipediaUrl null.
